blocks.content: I heard him before I turned around.
He was in the doorway. Pajamas. He should've been asleep twenty minutes ago.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: You were supposed to be asleep.
He came and sat down on the edge of the bed. He was quiet for a second in the way he gets quiet when he's carrying something he doesn't know how to say yet.

blocks.speaker: Larry Jr.
blocks.content: Is that the girl from the steps?
He'd known. He'd been carrying it since whenever he first overheard something. He just waited until now — the worst possible moment and somehow exactly the right one.
He said it the way kids say things — direct and complete. No hedging. No 'I mean I'm sure it'll work out.' Just: I'm sorry.
I reached over and put my hand on top of his head the way I used to when he was smaller.
